title: Caching & Performance Tuning

questions.question_text: What is the primary benefit of using a CDN (Content Delivery Network)?
options: It delivers assets from a server physically closer to the user to reduce latency
It replaces the need for a backend server
questions.explanation: By distributing your content across global servers, a CDN ensures that a user in Nairobi gets data from a nearby server instead of one in New York.

questions.question_text: What is a 'Cache' in web development?
options: A fast, temporary storage layer that holds data so future requests for that data can be served faster
questions.explanation: Caching avoids re-calculating or re-fetching data multiple times.

questions.question_text: What is the 'Cache-Control' header used for?
options: To control who can see the site
To tell browsers and CDNs how long they should store a copy of a file
questions.explanation: Setting a long 'max-age' for images and CSS improves site speed for returning visitors.

options: An open-source, in-memory data store frequently used as a database cache
questions.explanation: Redis is extremely fast because it stores everything in RAM instead of on a disk.

options: An automated tool for improving the quality of web pages (performance, SEO, accessibility)
questions.explanation: Lighthouse gives you a score and specific suggestions to make your site faster.

options: Breaking your code into multiple small files that are only loaded when needed
questions.explanation: This prevents users from downloading the 'Admin Dashboard' code when they are only visiting the 'Home' page.

options: A build process that removes unused (dead) code from your final bundle
questions.explanation: It 'shakes' the tree of your dependencies and only keeps the parts you actually used.

questions.question_text: What is a 'Bottleneck' in a system?
options: The slowest part of a system that limits the overall performance
questions.explanation: Common bottlenecks include slow database queries or unoptimized loops.

options: Taking a picture of the DB
The process of analyzing queries to find which ones are slow and need optimization (e.g., adding an index)
questions.explanation: Profiling helps you identify 'The N+1 Problem' and other efficiency issues.

questions.question_text: What is a 'Cold Start' in serverless functions?
options: The delay that occurs when a serverless function is triggered for the first time after being idle
questions.explanation: Serverless platforms spin down code when not in use; waking it up takes time.

options: Algorithms that shrink the size of your HTML/JS/CSS files during transmission
questions.explanation: Compressed files can be up to 70% smaller, leading to faster downloads.

questions.question_text: What is the 'Time to First Byte' (TTFB)?
options: How long it takes the user to click
The time it takes for the browser to receive the first byte of data from the server
The time to finish the build
questions.explanation: A high TTFB usually indicates slow server processing or database queries.

options: Storing the results of expensive function calls and returning the cached result when the same inputs occur again
questions.explanation: Memoization helps prevent unnecessary re-renders in React.

options: A network of servers located geographically close to the user (e.g., Vercel Edge, Cloudflare)
questions.explanation: Executing code 'at the edge' reduces latency significantly.

options: The process of declaring cached data as 'old' and forcing the system to fetch fresh data
questions.explanation: You should invalidate a cache when the underlying data in the database changes.

questions.question_text: True or False: More RAM always makes a slow database query faster.
options: False (You usually need better indexes or query logic)
questions.explanation: Scaling 'out' (more hardware) is often more expensive and less effective than scaling 'up' (better code and indexing).
